data.body:
PASS-1 review (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

Applicability: T8 fires only when the plan DEFINES an LLM/agent system (new prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, or enum vocabularies). This plan does not — it adds (1) a project-level disable-the-gate config (fail-open when off), (2) a written rollback/back-out + deploy-ordering runbook, and (3) an epic-spec cross-reference. It is a CONSUMER of the already-shipped signature/REVIEW_RESULT scheme, authoring no new prompt, decision protocol, counter, or enum.

Tool-grounded verification that every enum/schema/protocol the plan references is pre-existing and fully specified (so there is no undefined-but-referenced vocabulary):
- The signature verdict vocabulary (certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned) is fully defined in src/rebar/signing.py (verify_record) and src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json (enum).
- The REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is a pre-existing reducer-ignored, fsck-recognized event type (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44 `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`; docs/plan-review-gate.md:212-221) — the plan references it for rollout/coexistence, it does not define it.
- The disable config the plan references already ships: `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (src/rebar/config.py:260, default off; transition.py:651,661-662).

Structural-gap checklist — all pass for this chunk:
- (a) No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is never defined — verdict vocab + event types are defined in source.
- (b) No processing protocol/decision rule referenced but separated from its schema — the rollback decision rule sits with the config/event artifacts it governs; no LLM decision protocol is introduced.
- (c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement — none introduced (verify+transition is the existing atomic claim primitive).
- (d) No incomplete/failed sub-step lacking a fallback — the gate-off / fail-open behavior is itself the specified fallback path.
- (e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern — no new prompt text; all referenced surfaces (signing verify, REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, claim path) exist and are fully specified (tool-verified).

Affirmations (T8): not-applicable by construction (no new LLM/agent system defined); and where enum-like terms ARE referenced, each is tool-verified to exist and be fully specified in current source. No proven structural gap — PASS.
